caption: Step 1: Open eBay Advanced Search
narration: Let's start by opening eBay Advanced Search. It gives the walkthrough a clean starting point before the real automation work begins.

caption: Step 2: Enter main search keywords
narration: Next, we enter main search keywords. A little data entry here saves the same keystrokes on every future run.

caption: Step 3: Change the keyword match dropdown to exact order
description: Change the keyword match dropdown to exact order
narration: From there, we change the keyword match dropdown to exact order. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 4: Enter words to exclude from results
description: Enter words to exclude from results
narration: Now we enter words to exclude from results. A little data entry here saves the same keystrokes on every future run.

caption: Step 5: Include title and description in the search scope
description: Include title and description in the search scope
narration: After that, we include title and description in the search scope. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 6: Set minimum and maximum price filters
description: Set minimum and maximum price filters
narration: Next, we set minimum and maximum price filters. Being explicit here keeps reruns consistent.

caption: Step 7: Filter for new-condition items
narration: From there, we filter for new-condition items. It keeps the flow easy to follow without sounding like a checklist.

caption: Step 8: Run the search and open the filtered results page
description: Run the search and open the filtered results page
narration: Now we run the search and open the filtered results page. Once we run it, the page does the heavy lifting for us.
